free_text: High resolution investigation of the Sn-112(p,p')Sn-112
reaction.
A self-supporting metal foils stretched over an 8 mm
diameter hole in a tantalum target-frame. The thickness
was 190 mug/cm**2 for the Sn-112 and thickness of
160-mug/cm**2 for the natural target.
DWBA calculations.
An Enge split-pole spectrograph
six position sensitive solid state detector.
*EN-RSL-FW*.An energy resolution.
the AVF cyclotron of the Free
University in Amsterdam.
.Digitizing error of data.
By CAJAD-fig.2,3,4 of J,NP/A,333,116,1980
SB
Fig 4
Fig 2
.The relative error in the cross sections
results from the combination of the statistical error
as calculated by the peak-fitting program and a 3%
relative normalization error. This normalization error
accounts for uncertainties in the current-integration
system and for variations due to target
inhomogeneities.
Fig 3
.The relative error in the cross sections
results from the combination of the statistical error
as calculated by the peak-fitting program and a 3%
relative normalization error. This normalization error
accounts for uncertainties in the current-integration
system and for variations due to target
inhomogeneities.
